Oct. 13, 2024:
Dear Ms. Nolen: Below is the response dated Oct. 7, 2017 to your 'complaint'
in Oct. 2017. The fact that you have reposted the same complaint 7 years later indicates you are in need of mental health counseling.

Oct 7, 2017
Dear Ms. Nolen:

According to Wyde & Associates' (W&A) law firm records, your parents first retained the firm to represent you in July 2009 when you were charged with Evading Arrest or Detention and Public Intoxication. Due to my legal work, both charges were dismissed. A search of public record data bases reveals records of these two offenses no longer exist; which means these records are subject to court orders requiring all law enforcement agencies to either "expunge" (i.e: purge) or "non-disclose" (i.e: not to sell to public data bases) these records. I was happy to obtain those court orders on your behalf.

In May 2016 you were indicted by a Grand Jury for the extremely serious offense of Aggravated Assault with a Deadly Weapon, a 2nd degree felony (punishable by 2 to 20 years in prison or 2 to 10 years probation). You were also indicted for the offense of Criminal Mischief (destruction of property) of $2,500 to $30,000 (punishable by 180 days to 2 years --day for day (i.e: no good time credits) -- in a State Jail; or 2 to 5 years probation. Once again, your parents retained W&A to represent you. Due to my legal work on your behalf, the Aggravated Assault with a Deadly Weapon was dismissed--no conditions required--outright by State's attorney.

Unfortunately, the State's attorney refused to reduce the Criminal Mischief charge to a misdemeanor, or dismiss the charge. The prosecutor agreed to offer you deferred adjudication probation (non-conviction, which could be removed eventually from your criminal history) on the condition of you making restitution to the business whose property you damaged or destroyed. You refused to pay restitution, and therefore refused the plea offer from the prosecutor. I advised you that the only legal option you had left was to request a jury trial; as I advised you and your parents, if the case were tried to the judge, and not a jury, you would have been found guilty and ordered to pay the restitution. You refused to request or pay for the legal services necessary for a jury trial. Thereafter, you retained a different attorney--choosing to pay another attorney--instead of paying the restitution or for the legal services required to prepare and have a jury trial.

And, what was the result of your case? You received the exact plea agreement I originally negotiated for you --non conviction probation and you have been ordered to pay restitution to the business whose property was damaged or destroyed. Exactly what I previously advised you would happen.

I regret you are upset about how your subsequent attorney handled your case and the result he obtained for you. Furthermore, I never disclose to anyone the amount of fees W&A is paid, as that information is
privileged and confidential. I wish you the best of luck with future endeavors.

Respectfully,
Dan L. Wyde, Former Judge

Dear Ms. Masal:

Wyde & Associates never represented you and you were not a client of the firm. I did represent your father-in-law, who was falsely accused, and was able to prove his innocence to the State, and have the charges dismissed.
Upon his passing, his son (your husband) retained Wyde & Associates to resolve his father's estate matters. I was able to have your husband appointed independent executor of his father's estate, defeating your husband's step mother-in-law from being the executrix.
If you recall, the step mother-in-law unlawfully broke into your father-in-law's storage unit and stole many of his possessions. Your husband brought to my offices the possessions she did not steal, so an inventory could be filed with the probate court. Shortly thereafter, and prior to the inventory being completed, our offices were burglarized. Complaints were filed with the Dallas Police Dept. and the building's owner, Boxer Property. We searched for video recordings that may have captured the thieves who entered our offices but were not able to locate any relevant recordings. As there was no forced entry through the only door to our offices, we believe a member of the cleaning crew was responsible for the burglary. But without any video or witnesses, we could not prove who broke into the firm's offices.
As I explained to you on the phone and in person numerous times, we were not aware the personal items your husband brought to our offices were stolen until the inventory was prepared to be filed. I personally contacted and notified the State Bar of Texas to ascertain what, if anything, we could do. The State Bar advised us to inform the client (your husband) and file a police report, which we did and had updated.
Additionally, as you know, your step mother-in-law was fighting your husband to be the executrix of the estate. With every case we accept, a written agreement for legal services is provided to the client, and this was done with your husband. Pursuant to the agreement, I explained to you numerous times that there would be additional legal fees due to your step mother-in-law's attempts to obtain all of your father-in-law's estate property. Your husband (primarily due to your 'advice' chose not to pay any additional legal fees to fight his step mother-in-law in court. In fact, I specifically recall informing you on several occasions that she had the right to claim her share of the estate's community property. You refused to accept this legal fact, refused to compensate our law firm for the legal work that would be necessary, and your husband contractually agreed to pay to fight for his lawful share of his inheritance. You wanted our firm to work for free, and I again explained to you we could not, as we are a business and like all businesses we have expenses.
I regret our firm was not able to continue to represent your husband, but that was his and your choice, and I respect that. I sincerely hope the estate was eventually settled to your husband's satisfaction.
Respectfully,
Dan L. Wyde, Senior Judge

During the process, however, I was informed that Attorney Brittney was no longer with the law firm due to unprofessionalism and/or unjust practices. I then began working with Attorney Audra.  Everyone seemed to be nice and professional; however, after the firm accepted my payment and completed the affidavit, I noticed that the property was listed in my siblings' names and not mine.   I was informed by Attorney Audra that, due to Texas state laws, the property could not be transferred into my name. What is most frustrating is that this information—fundamental to my situation—was never disclosed to me beforehand. As a client, I trusted the attorney to provide clear guidance on whether my objective was even legally possible before taking my money. Hello? I am a nurse, not an attorney. This was the reason I sought legal advisement.  Instead, I was left with an incomplete solution that does not resolve the issue I initially sought help for. Clients deserve honesty and full disclosure upfront. While I understand that legal processes can be complex, failing to provide critical information until after payment creates the impression that the firm prioritizes fees over transparency. I would encourage anyone considering this firm to ask very direct questions and insist on written confirmation of what can and cannot be accomplished before moving forward.

My mom recently passed away and though she had a will, she did not have a trust or transfer on death deed (TODD) to pass on her house.

So I one I was headed for the dreaded “P,” (probate).  Like most would we were expecting a long drawn out and expensive probate process.

So under the stress of loss coupled with the unknown complexities of what we were about to get into, we searched for a good attorney in the Dallas area and quickly got an appointment with Audra Apke with Shutt Law Firm.

Like most people, we were expecting the worst as we’d read that to just get to a judge to be officially named the Executor per the will, it might take several months during which time I couldn’t put her house up for sale.  We live in a different county so the prospect of protecting/maintaining an empty house far away was daunting.

Audra asked us several questions about my mom’s financial and real assets and presented me with two options: normal probate with Letters Testamentary (the typical several month path) or an Affidavit of Heirship route, which could be executed relatively quickly.

She clearly laid out the pros and cons of each path.  After she did, it became obvious the AoH would be the best route for my mom’s real property.  Her financial assets already had beneficiaries named so no probate of any kind was necessary.

Within two months of my mom’s passing, we were able to put her house up for sale and sold it.

Absolutely amazing!!

Audra did an outstanding job clearly laying out the concerns for each party making it a very easy decision to go the path we did.

Oh and BTW, realizing our own estate had no transfer mechanism for our house (like a trust or a TODD), even though we lived on a different county in Texas, Audra prepared and got recorded in our county, a TODD for our house so our kids will not have to go through a complex legal process as they try to grieve our passing.

That Google search to find Shutt Law Firm and the appointment with Audra was the brightest shining light in a process that could have taken months!!

Audra is extremely easy to talk to, listens very well and clearly has the clients best interest in mind.  She reduced a complex legal process to something very understandable.

She made us very comfortable with our decision, and the quick sale of the house was proof her recommendation was correct!

If I could give Shutt Law Firm and Audra 6 stars, I would.

Without hesitation, I recommend them!!

reviews.text:
I recently had the pleasure of working with Mr. Salazar and his team for my spouse-based Green Card application, and I am compelled to share my extraordinary experience. Navigating the complexities of immigration law is a daunting task, but Mr. Salazar's expertise and unwavering support made the journey remarkably smooth.

From the outset, Mr. Salazar demonstrated a profound understanding of the U.S. immigration system. He was not just knowledgeable; he had a way of making complex legal jargon understandable for someone not versed in law. His approach was not merely professional but also highly personalized. He took the time to understand my unique situation, making me feel more like a valued client than just another case file.

The road to obtaining a Green Card is fraught with challenges, and my case was no exception. An unexpected error from the USCIS resulted in the initial denial of my application. This was a stressful moment, but Mr. Salazar's calm and confident demeanor was reassuring. He swiftly filed a petition to reopen my case, showcasing his remarkable ability to handle unforeseen complications with agility and expertise.

What truly set Mr. Salazar apart was his commitment to expedite my case. He formulated a compelling argument, highlighting the nuances of my situation and the error made by the USCIS. His argument was not only strong but also reflected a deep understanding of immigration law and policy. It was evident that he and his team had put in extensive research and effort into crafting a persuasive case.

Thanks to Mr. Salazar's diligence and skillful advocacy, my Green Card application was not just reopened but also approved. This outcome was beyond my expectations and speaks volumes about his proficiency and dedication to his clients.

In conclusion, I cannot recommend Mr. Salazar and his associates highly enough. Their support, knowledge, and effectiveness in handling complex immigration cases are exemplary. If you are seeking legal assistance for immigration-related matters, particularly a spouse-based Green Card, Mr. Salazar is undoubtedly the best choice. His services not only meet but exceed the highest standards of legal representation.

I wanted to take a moment to express my deepest gratitude for the outstanding legal service J Salazar Law Firm PC have  provided. Dedication, expertise, and unwavering commitment to my case have made a significant impact, and I am truly appreciative.

Throughout the entire process, Team's professionalism, attention to detail, and clear communication made a complex situation much more manageable. Team's efforts did not go unnoticed, and I am grateful for the positive outcome achieved under their guidance.

Thank you for going above and beyond to ensure the best possible resolution. The passion for work and commitment to your clients truly set your team apart. I feel fortunate to have had you representing me, and I will not hesitate to recommend your services to others in need of legal assistance.

I reached out to Nachawati Law Group on January 30, seeking representation for a workers' compensation case based on a friend's highly positive recommendation. Unfortunately, my experience was far from satisfactory.
After calling around 5:51 PM, I was transferred to an intake specialist. While she introduced herself at the beginning of the call, I struggled to retain her name due to a recent heart issue. When I asked for her name again, she refused to provide it. I inquired about when I could expect a follow-up call—whether Monday morning or afternoon—but felt as though my business was not valued. My frustration grew, and I expressed this clearly, yet she remained unhelpful and simply took my phone number before abruptly hanging up.
Feeling dismissed and disrespected, I decided to seek legal representation elsewhere, despite my understanding that Anne Saucer is a well-regarded attorney in this field. However, I want to acknowledge Timothy, who demonstrated professionalism and honesty during our interaction, even though he couldn't guarantee a callback time.
I followed up to ensure my contact information was noted, fearing it might have been lost due to the previous intake specialist's unprofessional attitude. Unfortunately, this experience has left me feeling disheartened about the service at Nachawati Law Group and will continue my search for a workers comp/ personal injury attorney.
This is a great law firm!
